title: Book of the Dead, Spell 135: Going Forth at the New Moon
source: Papyrus of Ani; Papyrus of Nu; various papyri
description: Spell 135 of the Egyptian Book of the Dead — the most extensive collection of funerary texts from any ancient civilization.
introduction: Spell 135 of the Egyptian Book of the Dead — the most extensive collection of funerary texts from any ancient civilization.

translation.text: From the Book of the Dead, Spell 135: 'Going Forth at the New Moon.' This spell empowered the deceased to navigate the dangers of the underworld and achieve eternal life among the gods. The full text was inscribed on papyrus and placed in the tomb.
